name: parcelLab Post-Purchase and Tracking API
what_it_does: parcelLab post-purchase experience and shipment tracking REST API for e-commerce brands to automate branded tracking pages, proactive delivery notifications, returns management, and customer communication across 150+ carriers worldwide — enabling retailers to own the post-purchase touchpoint and convert tracking page visits into repeat purchases. Enables AI agents to manage tracking data ingestion for carrier event automation, handle notification trigger for customer email/SMS automation, access tracking page for branded delivery portal automation, retrieve shipment analytics for delivery performance reporting automation, manage returns initiation for reverse logistics automation, handle exception handling for delay and failed delivery automation, access carrier event for delivery milestone automation, retrieve delivery prediction for ETA accuracy automation, manage order data for tracking context enrichment automation, and integrate parcelLab with Shopify, Salesforce Commerce, SAP, and ERP platforms for end-to-end post-purchase customer experience automation.
use_cases: Automating branded tracking page updates and delivery notifications for e-commerce customer experience agents
Triggering proactive shipment exception alerts for delay and failed delivery customer service automation agents
Analyzing delivery performance across carriers for logistics optimization and carrier selection automation agents
Managing returns portal and reverse logistics initiation for e-commerce post-purchase automation agents
not_for: Shipping label creation (parcelLab is tracking and post-purchase experience, not label generation)
Carrier rate comparison (parcelLab is downstream of carrier selection — tracking what's already shipped)
Small merchants with low shipment volumes (parcelLab targets mid-market and enterprise retailers)
best_when: A mid-market or enterprise e-commerce brand wanting AI agents to automate branded post-purchase communication, proactive delivery notifications, and tracking page personalization across 150+ carriers worldwide through parcelLab's experience platform.
avoid_when: REQUIRES DATA INGESTION SETUP: parcelLab must receive shipment data (tracking numbers + carrier + customer) to function; automated assumption of automatic data sync without integration creates empty tracking; automated must push shipment data to parcelLab on order fulfillment. PARTNER AGREEMENT REQUIRED: parcelLab targets mid-market and enterprise customers; automated self-service free API assumption creates sales qualification step; automated integration requires parcelLab commercial agreement. CARRIER COVERAGE VARIES: parcelLab supports 150+ carriers but not every carrier; automated assumption of global carrier coverage creates missing_carrier_events for unsupported carriers; automated should verify carrier support before commitment. NOTIFICATION TEMPLATES REQUIRE CONFIGURATION: parcelLab notification emails use templates configured in platform; automated out-of-box template assumption without configuration creates plain or generic notifications; automated must configure brand templates before go-live.

auth.notes: parcelLab uses API key (user + token header pair) for authentication. REST API with JSON. Munich, Germany HQ. Founded 2015 by Tobias Buxhoidt, Anton Eder, and Julian Krenge. Backed by Tiger Global, Insight Partners ($112M raised). Products: Branded tracking page, email/SMS notifications, returns portal, delivery analytics, carrier exception management. SDKs: None public. 150+ carrier integrations. 750+ retail clients. GDPR. Competes with AfterShip and Narvar for post-purchase experience.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Demo and sandbox for qualified prospects; production requires commercial agreement
pricing.paid_tiers: Subscription based on shipment volume; enterprise pricing; demo available on request
pricing.notes: Munich DE. Tiger Global backed. Volume-based subscription. Enterprise focus. GDPR.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; data ingestion throttled; webhooks for tracking events

known_agent_gotchas: SHIPMENT DATA MUST BE PUSHED: parcelLab does not pull tracking data automatically; automated passive tracking assumption creates no tracking events; automated must push shipment data (tracking_number, carrier, customer_email) to parcelLab API on order fulfillment

USER + TOKEN DUAL HEADER AUTH: parcelLab uses separate user and token headers (not a single API key); automated single API key header assumption creates 401; automated must send both user header and token header per request

CARRIER CODE MUST MATCH PARCELLAB ENUM: parcelLab uses specific carrier codes (e.g., 'dhl-germany', 'ups', 'fedex'); automated carrier name string creates carrier_not_found; automated must map own carrier names to parcelLab's carrier code list

TEMPLATE CONFIGURATION IS PRE-REQUISITE: Notification email templates must be configured in parcelLab platform before notifications fire; automated immediate notification expectation without template setup creates default/generic notification; automated must complete template setup before go-live

ENTERPRISE AGREEMENT REQUIRED: parcelLab targets mid-market/enterprise; automated self-service signup creates sales qualification step; automated integrations require completing parcelLab commercial onboarding
agent_readiness.security_notes: Post-purchase platform. GDPR, PCI-DSS. API key dual-header. EU/DE. Customer and shipment data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Post-purchase tracking. GDPR. API key. EU/DE. Customer email and shipment data.
